elements.name: Description
texts: "The general appearance of Templecarn as an agricultural district is wild and unpromising..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Parish of Templecarn, in Institute of Irish Studies, and Royal Irish Academy, Parishes of County Donegal / Edited by Angélique Day and Patrick McWilliams. II 1835-6, Mid, West and South Donegal., Ordnance Survey Memoirs of Ireland; v.39, p. 161

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Institute of Irish Studies in association with The Royal Irish Academy, Belfast

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "The general appearance of Templecarn as an agricultural district is wild and unpromising, except in the immediate vicinity of Pettigo and Lough Erne, the shores of which, being low and lying on limestone, are cultivated; neither is there any striking scenery unconnected with Lough Erne."
